meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn a professional client workflow to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real apps.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by simulating a professional client workflow. By treating your personal project as a real-world contract, you build production-grade software. Using Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to iterate, you transform abstract coding exercises into impressive, high-value case studies for your career.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen of your IDE, the weight of a non-existent portfolio pressing down on your career ambitions. Every job posting demands experience you haven't been given the chance to earn, and the 'tutorial hell' loop feels like a cage. You have the skills, but you lack the battle scars of a real client engagement.

It is time to stop building 'to-do lists' and start engineering solutions. By adopting a professional client workflow, you shift your identity from a student to an AI Builder. We will use advanced tooling to bridge the gap between amateur projects and professional-grade assets that command respect in any technical interview.

steps.title: Define the Client Requirements
steps.description: Treat your project like a paying contract. Document the business problem, user personas, and core technical requirements before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Create a PRD for a [Project Type] app. Outline 3 core features, user journey, and technical stack requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project-requirements.md file based on the PRD generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Professional apps require clean, scalable folder structures. Use Antigravity to generate a standard enterprise-level project boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable folder structure for a [Stack] application. Include modules for API routes, state management, and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the folder structure defined in project-requirements.md and initialize the repository.'

steps.title: Build the MVP Core
steps.description: Focus on the 'happy path' first. Use Claude Code to implement the primary function that solves the user's main pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for the [Feature Name] module, ensuring it handles edge cases and follows SOLID principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the core business logic for the primary feature and include basic unit tests.'

steps.title: Implement Professional Error Handling
steps.description: Amateurs write code that works; pros write code that fails gracefully. Add robust logging and error boundaries to your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error handling middleware for [Stack]. Include logging, user-friendly error messages, and fallback states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the existing code to include error handling middleware and logging as specified.'

steps.title: Generate Documentation and README
steps.description: Your portfolio piece is only as good as its documentation. A professional README is your most important marketing asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this project, including: Project Overview, Tech Stack, Setup Instructions, and Architecture Decisions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-quality README.md based on the project implementation.'

pro_tips: Always commit your code with 'feat:' or 'fix:' prefixes to demonstrate familiarity with Conventional Commits.
Add a 'Decision Log' file to your repo; explaining *why* you chose a tech stack is more impressive than the code itself.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your core logic; nothing signals 'senior-ready' like a passing test suite.
Deploy your project to a staging environment (like Vercel or Railway) immediately so you can include a live demo link in your resume.
Treat your GitHub repo as a product, not a storage locker; keep the commit history clean and meaningful.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, write a LinkedIn post detailing the 'client' problem you solved and the technical challenges you overcame. This transition from 'builder' to 'storyteller' is what lands interviews.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build a portfolio that reflects your actual potential, not just your current experience level. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
